concept_name: Lorentz Force on a Moving Charged Particle (F = q v × B)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.26 §26.2 (force on a moving charge in a magnetic field) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Diamond #2 of the magnetism chapter (M1, archetype B — force-in-field). The atomic concept being taught is *direction* (F is perpendicular to BOTH v and B via the right-hand palm rule, F reverses for negative charge) and *magnitude* (F = |q|vB sin θ, max at θ = 90°, zero when v ∥ B). The deepest insight is that F ⊥ v ALWAYS, so the magnetic force does no work, so |v| is conserved, so a charged particle in a uniform B describes a closed circle (or helix). The four most common student errors targeted here: F is along v, F is along B, F does work on the particle, and using the left hand for the cross-product. Diamond #2 establishes the renderer primitives for archetype B (ambient uniform field grid, moving particle, per-frame F = q v × B vector, palm-rule HTML overlay) so the rest of Ch.26 can be authored against them via the M3 patterns library. Pedagogy ordered per reviewer Asmi Singh (2026-06-16): 0° → 90° → 45° → 10° so the pure circle establishes 'why circular' before the helix is introduced.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture an old CRT television your grandparents kept in the corner of the living room — the kind with a deep glass back. Inside, an electron beam shoots from the back of the tube toward the front screen. Magnetic deflection coils around the neck of the tube create a B-field that steers the beam — line by line, top to bottom — across the phosphor screen so you see a picture. Without F = qv × B, the beam would land on one fixed spot and the screen would show only a dot.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the night sky over the Arctic during a geomagnetic storm — green and purple curtains of aurora light. Those colours come from solar-wind protons that smashed into Earth's magnetic field. The Lorentz force curved each proton along the field lines, funneling them down toward the poles. ISRO's satellites in low Earth orbit measure these particle streams continuously; the curving path is F = qv × B in real time.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture BARC's variable-energy cyclotron in Kolkata. Inside the vacuum chamber, a strong vertical B-field bends accelerated protons into a spiral path. Each lap, an alternating voltage gives them a kick of energy; the radius widens, the period stays the same, the proton spirals outward until it hits the target. The whole spiral is just the Lorentz force on a moving charge — the same physics as the CRT, just at MeV energies.

q_sign.name: Sign of the charge (positive or negative)

B.name: Magnitude of the uniform magnetic field

formulas.lorentz_force_vector: F = q (v × B) — F is perpendicular to both v and B; the cross product gives the direction (right-hand rule), q gives the sign.
formulas.lorentz_force_magnitude: F = |q| v B sin θ — maximum when v ⊥ B (θ = 90°), zero when v ∥ B (θ = 0° or 180°).
formulas.no_work_done: F · v = q (v × B) · v = 0 for every v. Magnetic force never changes kinetic energy; |v| is constant.
formulas.cyclotron_radius: r = m v_perp / (|q| B), where v_perp = v sin θ. The particle's circular component of motion has this radius.
formulas.cyclotron_period: T = 2π m / (|q| B). Independent of v — every charge of the same q/m takes the same time per loop.

constraints: B must be uniform — non-uniform fields produce more complex motion (e.g., magnetic mirrors).
Particle must be non-relativistic — at v close to c, the mass increases and r grows accordingly.
When v ∥ B (θ = 0), the cross product is zero — particle moves in a straight line, no force.
Cyclotron period T = 2π m / (|q| B) depends only on charge-to-mass ratio and B, not on v or r.
Magnetic force ALWAYS does zero work — speed |v| is conserved exactly, only direction changes.

aha_moment.statement: F is perpendicular to BOTH v and B. Cross product, never along either.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: At θ = 90° F is largest and always points to the centre of the circle. Tilt θ back toward 0 (STATE_5 → STATE_7) and the circle opens into a helix — but F stays at 90° to v the whole time.

STATE_1.title: θ = 0°: v parallel to B, no electric field → F = 0, straight line

scene_composition.text: θ = 0° — v is parallel to B

Uniform field B (blue arrows).
Electric field E = 0.
Orange arrow = v.

Lorentz force:
F = qE + q v×B
E = 0 → F = q v×B

θ = 0 → sin 0° = 0 → F = 0.
The proton drifts straight — no bending.

tts_sentences.text_en: Setup. The blue arrows are a uniform magnetic field B. There is no electric field here — E is zero.
The orange arrow is the proton's velocity v. Right now v points along B, so the angle theta is zero.
The full Lorentz force is q E plus q v cross B. With E zero, only the magnetic part is left.
Its size is q v B sine theta. With theta zero, sine zero is zero, so F is zero.
So the proton just drifts in a straight line — no bending.

STATE_2.title: θ = 90°: F maximum → full circle → r and T appear

scene_composition.text: θ = 90° — pure perpendicular, F at max

F = qvB · sin 90° = qvB
Full circle, no drift along B

Radius r = mv / (qB)
Period  T = 2π m / (qB)
Note: T does NOT depend on v
F ⊥ v at every instant → F·v = 0 → no work → |v| is constant. Trail dots are equally spaced — proof of constant speed.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now theta equals ninety. v is perpendicular to B.
Sine ninety equals one, so F reaches its maximum.
There is no drift along B now. The path is a perfect circle.
F always points to the centre — it is the centripetal force.
On the right, the 3D right-hand shows the rule. Fingers first, thumb last.
Point your fingers along v.
Curl them toward B.
Your thumb now gives F — pointing to the circle's centre.
Newton's second law for circular motion. The magnetic force is the centripetal force.
Solve for the radius.
And the period equals circumference divided by speed. The v cancels — T depends only on m, q, and B.
Notice the trail dots are equally spaced — equal arc per unit time.
F is always perpendicular to v, so F dot v is zero.
So the kinetic energy never changes. Magnetic forces do no work.

misconception_watch.belief: A magnetic field can speed up a charged particle.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Never. F · v = 0 by construction. Only electric fields can change |v|.

STATE_3.title: It turns, but the speed never changes — magnetic force does no work

scene_composition.text: Direction keeps changing — does the SPEED change too?

Speed |v| stays put — it NEVER changes.
Watch the trail: the dots are
equally spaced (equal distance, equal time).

The orange v-arrow swings to a
new direction every instant —
but its LENGTH stays the same.
F is always ⊥ v → F·v = 0 → work = 0 → kinetic energy can't change → speed is locked. A magnet can STEER a charge, never SPEED it up.

tts_sentences.text_en: The proton is going in a circle, so its direction is always changing.
Predict first. As it goes round and round, does its speed change?
Watch the trail. The dots are equally spaced — equal distance covered in equal time.
That means the speed is exactly constant. Only the direction turns.
Why? The force is always perpendicular to the velocity.
So the work done by the force is F dot v, and that is zero.
No work means the kinetic energy cannot change. So the speed is locked.
This is the big idea. A magnetic force only steers a charge — it can never speed it up or slow it down.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic force speeds the charge up — a stronger field makes it go faster.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Never. F is ⊥ v, so F·v = 0 and the work is zero. A bigger B makes a TIGHTER circle, not a faster particle. Only an electric field can change |v|.

STATE_4.title: Fleming's left-hand rule — Class-10 mnemonic, same physics

scene_composition.text: Fleming's left-hand rule — Class-10 reconciliation

Left hand:
ForeFinger = B (index)
seCond = v (middle)
thuMb = F

+q only.
For −q, reverse F.
For any θ ≠ 90°,
use right-hand rule.
Same physics as F = q v × B — three orthogonal fingers instead of a cross-product curl. Pick whichever hand you remember from Class 10.

tts_sentences.text_en: Quick bridge for Class-10 students. Some of you remember this as Fleming's left-hand rule from the motor effect chapter.
Index finger along B.
Middle finger along v.
Thumb along F.
For a positive charge, this gives the exact same force direction as the right-hand cross product.
We still prefer the right-hand rule because it works for any angle theta, and it handles negative charges without reversing.
Same physics — pick whichever hand you remember.

STATE_5.title: θ = 45°: tilt back from the circle → it opens into a helix

scene_composition.text: θ = 45° — tilt back from the circle

F = qvB · sin 45° ≈ 0.71 × max
(less than the full circle)

The closed circle OPENS into a helix —
part of v now drifts along B
F scales as sin θ. Coming down from 90°, the circle opens into a helix because part of v points along B. WHY sin θ? — next.

tts_sentences.text_en: We just saw the perfect circle at ninety degrees. Now tilt v back to forty-five degrees.
The Lorentz force is still here, but the path is no longer a closed circle — it opens into a helix.
F has dropped to seventy-one percent of its maximum. Sine forty-five is about zero point seven one.
Why a helix now? Because part of v points along B and drifts forward, while the rest circles around B.
Where does that sine theta come from?
Let's break v into two components — and the formula becomes obvious.

STATE_6.title: Why sin θ? — resolve v into v cos θ (∥ B) + v sin θ (⊥ B)

scene_composition.text: Why sin θ in F = qvB sin θ?

Grey arrow = v cos θ (∥ B)
Feels no force — but carries the
proton steadily FORWARD along B.

Orange dashed = v sin θ (⊥ B)
This makes F — and curls the path:
r = m(v sin θ)/(qB), T = 2π m/(qB)
F = q · v_⊥ · B = q · (v sin θ) · B → |F| = qvB sin θ. The sin θ in the formula IS the perpendicular slice — no magic.

tts_sentences.text_en: Time for the why. The formula has been working for us — but where does sine theta come from?
Break v into two pieces. The grey arrow is v cosine theta — the component along B.
The cross product of B with anything along B is zero, so v parallel makes no force.
But it never stops. v cosine theta carries the proton steadily forward along B — that is why the path keeps moving forward.
The orange dashed arrow is v sine theta — the component perpendicular to B.
This is the only part that creates F, and the only part that curls.
So F equals q times v perp times B — which is q v B sine theta.
The circle it makes has radius m v sine theta over q B. The period is two pi m over q B — the same as the full circle.
So sine theta is not magic. It is just the perpendicular slice of v.
On the right, the 3D right-hand shows the same gesture.
Fingers along v.
Curl toward B.
Thumb gives F.
The rule is identical for any angle.

misconception_watch.belief: F = qvB sin θ is a formula to memorise, with sin θ as an arbitrary correction.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: sin θ is just |v_⊥| / |v|. The formula is really F = q v_⊥ B. The component along B is irrelevant to the force; only the perpendicular slice creates force.

STATE_7.title: θ = 10°: almost parallel → a very stretched helix

scene_composition.text: θ = 10° — almost parallel to B

F = qvB · sin 10° ≈ 0.17 × max
(small, but NOT zero)

Very STRETCHED helix —
long forward drift, tiny circles ⊥ B
Push θ a little more to 0° and the circles vanish — back to the straight line we started with.

tts_sentences.text_en: Last case. Push theta all the way down to ten degrees — v is almost parallel to B.
F is still here, perpendicular to both v and B, but it is small.
Sine ten degrees is about zero point one seven, so F is only seventeen percent of its maximum.
The helix is now very stretched — long forward drift, tiny circles. Nudge theta to zero and the circles vanish: a straight line again.

STATE_8.title: Try it — drag the sliders, watch the path

scene_composition.text: You drive it now

Top-right: q sign · |v| · B · θ
F readout updates live

Try: θ = 0 → straight
θ = 90 → circle
In between → helix
Flip q → reverse sense
Same right-hand rule everywhere. Charge sign and θ are the two knobs that decide F.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Top-right has four sliders: q sign, speed v, field B, and angle theta. The F readout updates live as you drag.
Try theta zero — straight line.
Theta ninety — pure circle.
Anything in between — helix.
Tap the q button to flip charge sign and watch the trajectory reverse.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The magnetic force F points in the same direction as v — the field pushes the particle forward.
trigger_phrases: force is in the direction of motion
magnetic force pushes the particle forward

epic_c_branches.misconception: The magnetic force F points in the same direction as B — it just shoves the charge along the field.
trigger_phrases: force is in the direction of the field
magnetic force points along magnetic field

epic_c_branches.misconception: A magnetic field can speed up or slow down a charged particle — magnetic force does work like any other force.
trigger_phrases: B field speeds up the charge

epic_c_branches.misconception: Use the left hand for the magnetic force direction — that is the natural hand for cross products in physics.

STATE_1.label: θ = 0° — v ∥ B, straight drift, F = 0

STATE_2.label: θ = 90° — pure circle, r and T appear

STATE_3.label: Speed constant — magnetic force does no work
STATE_3.formula_overlay:
F ⊥ v  →  F·v = 0
W = 0  →  Δ(½mv²) = 0
|v| = constant

STATE_5.label: θ = 45° — tilt back from the circle, it opens into a helix

STATE_6.label: Why sin θ — v decomposed into v cos θ (∥ B) + v sin θ (⊥ B)

STATE_7.label: θ = 10° — very stretched helix, nearly back to straight

STATE_8.label: Interactive — drag sliders, flip q sign
STATE_8.formula_overlay:
F = qvB sin θ
r = mv / (qB)
T = 2π m / (qB)
